evidence: Double solid yellow center lines indicate right-hand traffic and are standard in Brazil and other American countries.
The presence of reddish soil (Terra Roxa) is a very strong indicator for Brazil, particularly in the states of Paraná, São Paulo, and Mato Grosso do Sul.
The metal lattice electricity pylon is a common infrastructure feature in rural Brazil.
Flat farmland with large deciduous trees is consistent with the agricultural landscapes of Southern and Central Brazil.
The white camera car is a frequent observation in Brazilian Google Street View coverage.
contradictions: While Mexico and the US also use double yellow lines, the specific combination with highly reddish soil is most characteristic of Brazil.
